input.title: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #67244: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 67244
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/67244
- Title: Explicit ACP agent runs: embedded backend visibility failure and stale final JSON state after sessions_yield
- State: OPEN
- Author: sazora
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
OpenClaw explicit `agent --json` ACP runs currently hit two separate bugs in the embedded explicit-run path. Both are fixed locally for now, but only as hotfixes in installed Homebrew `dist` files.

## Environment

- OpenClaw installed via Homebrew
- Patched runtime files currently under `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/`
- Repro workspace: `/Users/stevebot/.openclaw/workspace`
- Repro target path: `/Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business`

## Repro command

```bash
openclaw agent \
  --session-id 'agent:main:discord:channel:1493603713426788494' \
  --message 'Use Codex via ACP. Create exactly one file at /Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business/oc-acp-write-test-smoke.txt containing exactly: ACP SSD smoke test ok.' \
  --json \
  --timeout 180
```

Optional verification:

```bash
cat /Volumes/4TB-SSD/Projects/wrap-business/oc-acp-write-test-smoke.txt
```

## Expected behavior

- The explicit ACP run should succeed when the embedded ACP backend is healthy.
- The target file should be created with the requested contents.
- Final JSON should reflect the completed state, including:
  - `status: "ok"`
  - `finalAssistantVisibleText`
  - `replayInvalid: false`
  - `livenessState: "completed"`

## Actual behavior

Two different failures were observed:

1. **Embedded explicit ACP backend visibility failure**
   - Explicit `agent --json` ACP runs could fail with:
     - `ACP runtime backend is not configured. Install and enable the acpx runtime plugin.`
   - At the same time, gateway logs showed the backend was already healthy, e.g.:
     - `embedded acpx runtime backend ready`

2. **Stale final JSON state after `sessions_yield`**
   - Even when the ACP descendant work completed successfully and the file existed on disk, explicit `agent --json` could still return stale metadata such as:
     - `replayInvalid: true`
     - `livenessState: "working"`

## Root cause summary

### Bug 1: explicit embedded process could not see ACP backend
ACP backend registration was process-local. The gateway process had registered the embedded ACPX backend, but the explicit embedded run process performed backend lookup without ensuring local registration first.

### Bug 2: explicit final-state reconciliation depended on initial assistant text
In the explicit agent RPC path, descendant completion reconciliation after `sessions_yield` was gated on `initialAssistantText`. If the parent yielded before producing assistant-visible text, reconciliation was skipped, leaving stale

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.title: [Feature]: Add provider-specific TTS prompt hints
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #67539: [Feature]: Add provider-specific TTS prompt hints
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 67539
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/67539
- Title: [Feature]: Add provider-specific TTS prompt hints
- State: OPEN
- Author: barronlroth
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Add provider-specific, model-aware TTS prompt hints so agents know which expressive controls are valid for the active speech provider.

## Problem to solve

OpenClaw already has a generic TTS runtime hint that tells agents they can use `[[tts:...]]` directives and optional `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` blocks. That is enough for the transport layer, but it does not teach the model provider-specific expressive syntax.

For example, the Google Gemini TTS provider added in #67515 passes text directly to Gemini, so square-bracket audio tags such as `[whispers]` or `[laughs]` work when they appear inside the spoken TTS text. However, the active system hint does not tell the agent that Gemini supports those tags, so the feature is discoverable in docs but not reliably discoverable at runtime.

This is not unique to Gemini. Different TTS providers and models have different expressive surfaces: Gemini audio tags, ElevenLabs model-specific performance cues, OpenAI model-specific instructions support, Microsoft voice/rate/pitch behavior, etc. Without provider-scoped hints, agents may either miss useful expressive controls or mix syntax from one provider into another.

## Proposed solution

Add an optional provider-owned prompt hint hook to the speech provider contract, for example:

```ts
type SpeechProviderPlugin = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  buildPromptHint?: (ctx: {
    model: string;
    voice?: string;
    providerConfig: SpeechProviderConfig;
    modelOverrides: ResolvedTtsModelOverrides;
    target: "audio-file" | "voice-note";
  }) => string | undefined;
};
```

Then have `speech-core` append the active provider's hint from `buildTtsSystemPromptHint` when TTS is enabled.

Design expectations:

- Keep the existing generic TTS hint in `speech-core`.
- Inject only the selected provider's hint, not every registered provider's syntax.
- Let providers branch internally by resolved model, voice, output target, or provider config.
- Respect `modelOverrides` policy, so hints do not advertise controls the model is not allowed to emit.
- Keep hints short enough for normal system prompt injection.
- Add tests that verify only the active provider hint is injected and disabled/off-policy controls are not advertised.

For Gemini, a provider hint could be as small as:

```text
Google Gemini TTS supports expressive square-bracket cues inside spoken text, such as [whispers], [laughs], or [cheerfully]. Put them inside [[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]] when they 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

**Summary**

input.title: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #68725: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: pull_request
- Number: 68725
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68725
- Title: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
- State: OPEN
- Author: wirjo
- Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
# feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models

## Problem

Mantle's `/v1/models` endpoint returns only model IDs — no token limit metadata. Discovery hardcodes `contextWindow: 32000` for every model, which is wrong for most:

- MiniMax M2/M2.1: **1,000,000** (gets 32K)
- Qwen3 Coder: **256,000** (gets 32K)
- DeepSeek V3.x, GLM 4.x, Nemotron: **128,000** (gets 32K)

This causes the same premature context overflow and wrong compaction thresholds as #65952 (the amazon-bedrock equivalent).

## Fix

Add a `KNOWN_CONTEXT_WINDOWS` lookup table for open-weight models currently available on Mantle. Raise the default fallback from 32K → 128K for unknown models.

```
MiniMax M2 / M2.1              → 1,000,000
Qwen3 Coder (480B/Next/30B)    → 256,000
Qwen3 235B / 32B               → 128,000
DeepSeek V3.1 / V3.2           → 128,000
GLM 4.6 / 4.7 / 4.7 Flash      → 128,000
NVIDIA Nemotron (all variants)  → 128,000
```

**Claude models are intentionally excluded** — they use a separate Anthropic-native endpoint on Mantle (`/anthropic/v1/messages`), not the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint that this extension handles. Claude model metadata is managed by the `amazon-bedrock` extension (#65952).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts` | +41/-2 — context window map, `resolveKnownContextWindow()`, default bump |

Single file, no type system changes.

## References

- [Mantle model list](https://conoroneill.net/2026/02/20/aws-bedrock-mantle-model-names-youve-been-looking-for/) (community, Feb 2026)
- [Opus 4.7 model card](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-card-anthropic-claude-opus-4-7.html) — confirms Claude uses `/anthropic/v1/messages` on Mantle, not Chat Completions
- Related: #65952 (same pattern for amazon-bedrock discovery)

Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof
```

Author: kesslerio - Labels: size: M, proof: supplied, P2, rating: 🦪 silver shellfish, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 5 - Changed files: src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts, src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts, src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary - Problem: bound ACP sessions stayed attached after the runtime `cwd` disappeared, so retries kept failing against a dead session. - Why it matters: affected threads remain wedged until someone manually clears the stale binding. - What changed: treat missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors as stale-session signals in both dispatch-time cleanup and reset-in-place cleanup, and cover both paths with regressions. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): stale cleanup still only triggers for `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; this does not broaden cleanup to generic turn failures. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - Root cause: stale-session detection only recognized metadata and resource-not-found init failures, so a missing ACP runtime working directory did not trigger stale unbind. - Missing detection / guardrail: reset-in-place failure handling returned the error without clearing the stale binding, and no regression covered missing-`cwd` init failures. - Contributing context (if known): the old fix branch had drifted too far from current `main`, but the bug still reproduced on April 18, 2026. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`, `src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors unbind stale bindings during both dispatch and reset-in-place recovery. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: both failure paths are local runtime/control-plane seams and do not need a live ACP backend to prove the cleanup behavior. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Bound ACP conversations recover automatically when the runtime working directory is gone instead of staying wedged behind a stale binding. ## D [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-19T06:34:24Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes stale ACP bound sessions that remained attached when the runtime working directory (`cwd`) was gone. Both new code paths are covered by regression tests.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the logic is correct, scope is tightly constrained to ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED, and both affected code paths have regression coverage. Only a P2 style finding (duplicated unbind-reason string) remains; no correctness, data-integrity, or reliability issues were identified. No files require special attention.

sessionMetaMocks.readAcpSessionEntry.mockReset().mockReturnValue(undefined); resolveMocks.resolveConfiguredAcpBindingSpecBySessionKey.mockReset().mockReturnValue(null); ({ ensureConfiguredAcpBindingSession, resetAcpSessionInPlace } = @@ -190,8 +201,6 @@ describe("ensureConfiguredAcpBindingSession", () => { expect(initializeArgs.agent).toBe("codex"); input.title: fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #69256: fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 69256 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/69256 - Title: fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running - State: OPEN - Author: lllyin - Labels: docs, size: S, triage: refactor-only, clawsweeper, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 4 - Changed files: CHANGELOG.md, docs/automation/cron-jobs.md, src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts, src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary - **Problem**: Isolated cron sessions with `deleteAfterRun: true` are being deleted prematurely during a run if a "Direct Delivery" (structured content or threaded reply) is triggered, even if subagents (workers) are still active. - **Why it matters**: In orchestrator-worker patterns, the parent agent must survive to receive and aggregate results from its subagents. Premature cleanup orphans the workers and creates a fresh, context-less session, breaking the pipeline. - **What changed**: Modified `cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded` in `delivery-dispatch.ts` to check `countActiveDescendantRuns` before executing `sessions.delete`. - **What did NOT change**: The cleanup logic for standard text-only deliveries (which already had subagent awareness via `finalizeTextDelivery`) was not altered. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #67807 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) - **Root cause**: PR #67807 introduced automated session cleanup in the `finally` block of `deliverViaDirectAndCleanup` but missed the subagent-counting guardrail that exists in the sibling `finalizeTextDelivery` path. - **Missing detection / guardrail**: Lack of `countActiveDescendantRuns` verification in the Direct Delivery cleanup wrapper. - **Contributing context**: Complex workflows using Feishu or threaded replies trigger the `useDirectDelivery` path, bypassing the subagent waiting orchestration. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - Target test or file: `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: A cron job with `deleteAfterRun: true` that spawns a subagent and returns structured content should NOT trigger `sessions.delete` until the subagent completes. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It directly asserts that the gateway call is suppressed in the presence of active runs. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes None. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Bef [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-20T07:35:15Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a subagent-count guard to `cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded` in `delivery-dispatch.ts`, mirroring the check that already existed in `finalizeTextDelivery`. When `countActiveDescendantRuns` returns a positive value, the `sessions.delete` call is skipped and the session is preserved for worker completion events, preventing orphaned workers in orchestrator-worker cron pipelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is minimal, well-targeted, and consistent with the sibling text-delivery path's existing pattern. The change is a one-for-one port of the guard already present in finalizeTextDelivery into cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded. No new logic is introduced; the only new behaviour is a conditional early-return that keeps the session alive while workers are running. The session will still be deleted on a future invocation once countActiveDescendantRuns returns 0, so there is no session-leak risk. All remaining findings are P2 or lower. No files require special attention. Fixes #68656. input.title: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #69260: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 69260
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69260
- Title: Harden Gemini ACP integration against ambient API-key fallback and add generic auth-contract hooks for ACP agents
- State: OPEN
- Author: anyech
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, clawsweeper:linked-pr-open, impact:security, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary
This is an integration-hardening / defense-in-depth issue, not a claim that OpenClaw is the sole root cause of Gemini auth drift.

We just finished a local incident/debugging cycle where a risky Gemini ACP path could drift durable Gemini auth away from `oauth-personal` and into the API-key lane when `GEMINI_API_KEY` was ambient.

The deepest bug seam appears to belong upstream in Gemini CLI, because ACP auth handling seems able to persist a fallback auth method back into durable Gemini settings.

However, OpenClaw also contributed to the exposure: the ACP launch/runtime path allowed provider auth env to be inherited broadly. That is a defensible default in some environments, but in a mixed-credential host it makes the wrong auth lane eligible unless the integration hardens against it explicitly.

### What local stage validation showed
We ran an isolated-HOME comparison without touching live user state.

#### Old risky path
- no explicit outer auth selector for the Gemini ACP lane
- bare Gemini ACP launch path
- ambient `GEMINI_API_KEY`

Result:
- the staged auth state drifted to the API-key lane

#### Hardened local path
- Gemini durable settings pinned to `oauth-personal`
- explicit outer selection guard for the Gemini ACP lane
- Gemini launch path strips Gemini / GenAI auth env before exec
- gateway/runtime no longer carries broad Gemini auth env by default

Result:
- the same staged pressure did not drift the auth state back

### Why file this in OpenClaw
Even if the deepest fix lands upstream in Gemini CLI, OpenClaw still has useful product-level hardening work here.

The lesson is broader than Gemini:

> ACP-backed agent integrations that depend on a specific auth mode should be able to declare that auth contract explicitly, scrub incompatible ambient auth env, and fail closed when the contract is not met.

### Suggested product direction
#### 1. Auth-contract metadata on ACP agent definitions
Allow an ACP-backed agent or route to declare:
- expected auth mode
- whether fallback auth methods are allowed
- whether ambient provider auth env should be stripped

#### 2. Integration-level env scrubbing hooks
Provide a first-class way to remove provider auth env from ACP launches when a route is supposed to use another auth surface.

For example, Gemini ACP routes should be able to scrub things like:
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI`
- `GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA`

#### 3. Fail-closed prefligh

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.title: ACP: keep thread-bound follow-ups parent-orchestrated by default, not raw pass-through
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #69669: ACP: keep thread-bound follow-ups parent-orchestrated by default, not raw pass-through
input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 69669
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69669
- Title: ACP: keep thread-bound follow-ups parent-orchestrated by default, not raw pass-through
- State: OPEN
- Author: V0hgg
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary
Thread-bound ACP follow-ups are currently too easy to treat as raw pass-through to the child session, instead of remaining parent-orchestrated by default.

In practice, the first prompt is often synthesized correctly by the parent assistant, but later shorthand follow-ups like:
- "ừa bắt nó làm tiếp như m suggest đi"
- "ok triển khai phase 2 đi"
- "bảo nó làm tiếp theo hướng đó"

can end up being forwarded as-is to the ACP child harness.

## Expected behavior
Thread binding should preserve child session continuity, but **not** imply raw-message forwarding semantics.

Default behavior should be:
1. parent assistant reads the user's shorthand follow-up
2. parent resolves references from parent-thread context
3. parent rewrites the follow-up into an explicit child task
4. parent sends the rewritten task to the bound ACP child session

Raw verbatim relay should happen **only** when the user explicitly asks for exact forwarding.

## Why this matters
Users often speak to the parent orchestrator in shorthand. If the raw message is passed through directly, the child receives low-context prompts and the orchestration UX breaks down.

This is especially visible in ACP thread-bound coding workflows where the user expects:
- user message -> parent interprets and expands
- parent prompt -> child executes

not:
- user message -> raw forward -> child guesses

## Evidence / current architecture notes
Observed locally:
- initial ACP thread spawn used `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", thread=true, mode="session")`
- first child prompt was explicitly synthesized by the parent
- later shorthand follow-up was effectively treated as pass-through
- child saw the raw user text rather than a rewritten explicit task

Potentially relevant code paths:
- `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`
- `src/gateway/session-subagent-reactivation.ts`
- thread-bound session continuation / reactivation behavior after `sessions.send`

The current accepted note for ACP session spawns says:
> "thread-bound ACP session stays active after this task; continue in-thread for follow-ups."

That note is correct for continuity, but ambiguous enough that it can lead to a pass-through mental model in parent orchestration.

## Proposed direction
- Keep thread-bound ACP continuity
- Clarify policy: continuity != verbatim relay
- Ensure parent remains the default interpreter/orchestrator for follow-ups
- Only relay exact raw messages when the user explicitly requests verbatim forwarding

## Acceptance 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

input.github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 71216
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/71216
- Title: Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`
- State: OPEN
- Author: newbrevity
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, impact:session-state, impact:security, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
# Proposed upstream issue — openclaw/openclaw

**Title:** Config schema: add `sandbox`, `routing.rules`, `instances`, and `gateway.nodes.denyPaths`

---

## Context

Running OpenClaw v2026.4.22 on macOS (Mac Studio M4 Max) as a single-user local gateway at `127.0.0.1:18789`. Using it for a mixed cloud / local-Ollama setup with sensitivity-based routing.

The current schema covers auth, bind, tailscale, controlUi, `gateway.nodes.denyCommands`, and `session.dmScope`. Four adjacent capabilities are not in the schema and would meaningfully reduce wrapper / sidecar code.

## Asks

1. **`sandbox.mode`** — global sandbox mode for tool execution. At minimum two values: `main` (current behavior) and `non-main` (sensitive operations refuse outside an approval flow). Today `appServer.mode: guardian` exists but is Codex-plugin-scoped.

2. **`routing.rules`** — tag-based provider selection. Useful shape:
   ```json
   {
     "routing": {
       "defaults": { "provider": "anthropic" },
       "rules": [
         {
           "match": { "tags": ["sensitive", "personal-finance"] },
           "provider": "ollama",
           "host": "127.0.0.1:11434",
           "model": "llama3:70b"
         }
       ]
     }
   }
   ```
   Any skill/agent call carrying one of the tags routes to the matching provider. Today this has to be enforced by a skill-level library that reads a policy sidecar.

3. **`instances.*`** — multi-instance awareness in one config so multiple gateways on the same host (one per user) can be declared and validated together, with per-instance workspace + port + active/deferred state. Today each instance needs a separate config file and wrapper-enforced isolation.

4. **`gateway.nodes.denyPaths`** — symmetric with the existing `denyCommands` array. A list of absolute paths/prefixes the gateway refuses to read or write regardless of which tool is asking. Covers credentials dirs, SSH keys, secrets archives.

## Why it matters

Without these, the policy gets split across the config, a sidecar JSON file, and per-skill code. Any skill that forgets the guard silently falls back to the default provider or touches a denyPath. Moving the four capabilities into the gateway would let `openclaw doctor` validate them and the gateway enforce them at one chokepoint.

## Happy to help

Willing to draft schema PRs for (1) and (4) if the project agrees the shape. (2) and (3) have more design surface and probably benefit from maintainer direction first.

---

**Environment**

- 

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

- Fresh-review commands do not start repair, autofix, rebase, CI repair, or automerge.
- Maintainer-only repair and merge flows require explicit commands such as `@clawsweeper autofix`, `@clawsweeper automerge`, `@clawsweeper fix ci`, or `@clawsweeper address review`.
- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

**Summary**
Keep open: current main still lacks the requested public config contracts, and the overlapping filesystem ACL and multi-instance discussions cover only

per_label_rationale.label: memory
per_label_rationale.rationale: Fixes the session-memory summarizer so reset archives are read into the daily memory file instead of being skipped.

per_label_rationale.label: sessions
per_label_rationale.rationale: Archived reset/deleted session transcript files and reset transcript lookup are central to the change.

per_label_rationale.label: hooks
per_label_rationale.rationale: The implementation changes the bundled session-memory hook behavior that runs at reset time.

per_label_rationale.label: skills_plugins
per_label_rationale.rationale: The session-logs skill surface is updated with helpers and examples for archived transcript forms.

per_label_rationale.label: docs
per_label_rationale.rationale: A central part of the PR is updating SKILL.md guidance and command examples for session logs.

excluded_label_rationale.label: gateway
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: The gateway rename behavior motivates the issue, but gateway routes/startup/state are not changed.

excluded_label_rationale.label: reliability
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: The PR uses recovery/loss language, but the deliverables are session transcript lookup, memory hook behavior, and skill documentation rather than a general operational reliability mechanism.

excluded_label_rationale.label: tests_ci
excluded_label_rationale.rationale: Tests are added for the new paths, but test infrastructure is not the subject.

ambiguity.why_not_hard: The two central surfaces are explicit: session-memory hook recovery and session-logs skill documentation. Ambiguity is limited to co-label boundaries for hooks/docs/sessions, not the core routing.

input.title: Recover archived (.reset) session transcripts in memory hook + session-logs skill
input.target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #71537: Recover archived (.reset) session transcripts in memory hook + session-logs skill
input.github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 71537 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/71537 - Title: Recover archived (.reset) session transcripts in memory hook + session-logs skill - State: OPEN - Author: injinj - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 4 - Changed files: pnpm-lock.yaml, skills/session-logs/SKILL.md, src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts, src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary When a session is reset (`/new` or `/reset`), the gateway renames its `.jsonl` transcript to `.jsonl.reset.Z`. After that rename, two surfaces silently lose the conversation: 1. **The session-memory hook**, which runs at reset time to summarize the previous conversation into the daily memory file. It only ever looked at live `.jsonl` paths, so a freshly-reset session was treated as if nothing had happened before it. 2. **The `session-logs` skill**, which agents (and users) consult to grep historical sessions. Every example used a `*.jsonl` glob and quietly skipped both `.reset.*Z` and `.deleted.*Z` archives. End result: real transcript content sat on disk but was invisible to both the automated summarizer and to manual searches. This branch fixes both halves so archives stop being a black hole. ## Changes ### 1. `fix(session-memory): recover archived reset transcripts` `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` - `getRecentSessionContentWithResetFallback` now scans the sessions directory for `.reset.Z` siblings and reads the newest archive when the live transcript is empty/missing. - `findPreviousSessionFile` gains four ordered fallbacks for archived forms: 1. \`currentSessionFile\` is itself a \`.reset.*Z\` archive 2. \`.jsonl.reset.*Z\` exists for the requested id 3. \`-topic-...jsonl.reset.*Z\` topic-variant archives 4. Any \`.jsonl.reset.*Z\` in the directory (newest wins) - All existing live-\`.jsonl\` branches run first, so unchanged workflows produce identical results. \`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts\` adds three test cases covering: a \`.reset.*Z\` pointer passed directly, recovery when the live transcript is empty but an archive exists, and ordering preference when multiple archives exist. ### 2. \`docs(session-logs): include archived transcripts in skill examples\` \`skills/session-logs/SKILL.md\` - Documents the three on-disk filename forms (\`*.jsonl\`, \`*.jsonl.reset.*Z\`, \`*.jsonl.deleted.*Z\`) and warns that plain globs miss the archived ones. - Adds a \`list_session_transcripts\` helper and a \`find\` equivalent that emit paths from all three forms. - Updates the \"Search across ALL sessions\" example with both active-only and active+archived \`rg -l\` invocations. - Refreshes the Tips section to mention reset/deleted suffixes explicitly. ## Why both in one PR These changes are two halves of the sam [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-25T10:47:30Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two surfaces that silently ignored archived (`.reset.*Z`, `.deleted.*Z`) session transcripts: `findPreviousSessionFile` gains four ordered fallback branches to surface reset archives when no live `.jsonl` is available, and `getRecentSessionContentWithResetFallback` gains a directory scan to recover content from the newest archive when the primary file is empty. The `session-logs` skill docs are updated with working glob/`find`/`rg` snippets that include the archived forms. The implementation logic is sound, test coverage for the new paths is thorough, and the priority ordering (live file > canonical reset > topic-reset > directory-wide newest) is correctly applied.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge; only P2 documentation style concerns remain. No P0 or P1 issues found. The two P2 findings are confined to the SKILL.md documentation snippet: `shopt -s nullglob` leaks into the caller's shell environment, and the `for f in $(list_session_transcripts)` tip is word-split-unsafe for paths with spaces. Core TypeScript logic and tests are correct. skills/session-logs/SKILL.md — minor bash hygiene in the helper snippet.

expect(memoryContent).toContain("user: Message from reset pointer"); expect(memoryContent).toContain("assistant: Recovered directly from reset file"); @@ -463,6 +463,40 @@ describe("session-memory hook", () => { expect(memoryContent).toContain("assistant: Recovered by sessionId fallback"); + const { sessionsDir } = await createSessionMemoryWorkspace(); + const sessionId = "reset-only-session"; + dir: sessionsDir, + name: `${sessionId}.jsonl.reset.2026-02-16T22-26-33.000Z`, + content: createMockSessionContent([ + { role: "user", content: "Older archived session" }, + dir: sessionsDir, + name: `${sessionId}.jsonl.reset.2026-02-16T22-26-34.000Z`, + content: createMockSessionContent([ + { role: "user", content: "Newest archived session" }, + const previousSessionFile = await findPreviousSessionFile({ + sessionsDir, + sessionId, + expect(previousSessionFile).toBe(newerResetFile); + (name) => name.startsWith(`${trimmedSessionId}-topic-`) && name.includes(".jsonl.reset."), + return path.join(params.sessionsDir, topicResetVariants[0]); if (!params.currentSessionFile) { @@ -141,6 +163,14 @@ export async function findPreviousSessionFile(params: { return path.join(params.sessionsDir, nonResetJsonl[0]); + return path.join(params.sessionsDir, resetJsonl[0]); Subject: [PATCH 2/4] docs(session-logs): include archived transcripts in skill a list_session_transcripts helper so searches can opt in to full Pairs with the session-memory recover-from-archive fix in the same --- skills/session-logs/SKILL.md | 49 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++- diff --git a/skills/session-logs/SKILL.md b/skills/session-logs/SKILL.md --- a/skills/session-logs/SKILL.md +++ b/skills/session-logs/SKILL.md @@ -44,6 +44,13 @@ Use the `agent=` value from the system prompt Runtime line. - **`sessions.json`** - Index mapping session keys to session IDs - **`.jsonl`** - Full conversation transcript per session +- **`.jsonl.reset.Z`** - Transcript archived by `/new` or `/reset` +- **`.jsonl.deleted.Z`** - Transcript archived when a session was deleted @@ -57,6 +64,30 @@ Each `.jsonl` file contains messages with: +SESSION_DIR="${OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.openclaw}/agents/$AGENT_ID/sessions" +list_session_transcripts() { + for f in "$SESSION_DIR"/*.jsonl \ + "$SESSION_DIR"/*.jsonl.reset.*Z [diff selected/truncated after 5000 chars] ```
